denial_pattern_sources: plan_exclusion_overrides_state_mandate: NCSL State Health Insurance Mandates Database 2024; documented in CA, NJ, IL enforcement actions

denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

This letter advises you of our determination regarding your provider's request on 03/13/2026 for Electrophysiology study with ablation related to Wolff-Parkinson-White pattern with SVT (I45.6). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) criteria and InterQual review standards. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Emergency imaging/treatment for Wolff-Parkinson-White pattern with SVT (I45.6) is denied for lack of prior authorization. Retroactive authorization is not granted for non-emergent services.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with emergency retroactive auth. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
clinical_context: The 19-year-old member has Wolff-Parkinson-White pattern with SVT (I45.6). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Electrophysiology study with ablation recommended as the next appropriate step. Monthly palpitation episodes; delta wave on resting ECG. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that emergency retroactive auth bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: The brief matches the assigned Aetna / cardiology cell and executes sub_tactic 'emergency_retroactive_auth' with a clinically appropriate Wolff-Parkinson-White pattern with SVT (I45.6) → Electrophysiology study with ablation pair.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Diagnosis, prior treatments, and insurer administrative posture reflect everyday commercial UM denials rather than textbook perfection.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Aetna correspondence with CPB and InterQual fingerprints and procedural tone.
